learn.title: Dividing by 2
learn.content: Dividing by 2 is the same as finding half. 10 ÷ 2 = 5 because half of 10 is 5. You split one group into exactly 2 equal parts.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.1 'Divide by 2' pp.265-268
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Halving and Doubling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Use Doubles
learn.content: If you know 2 × 8 = 16, then 16 ÷ 2 = 8. Doubles facts help you divide by 2 quickly. Think: 2 × ? = 16 → ? = 8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.1 'Divide by 2' pp.265-268
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Halving and Doubling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Model It with Two Groups
learn.content: To find 14 ÷ 2: draw 2 circles. Deal 14 counters one by one, alternating circles. Each circle gets 7. So 14 ÷ 2 = 7.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.1 'Divide by 2' pp.265-268
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Halving and Doubling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Halving on a Number Line
learn.content: On a number line, 12 ÷ 2 = 6 means: start at 12, make jumps of 2 going back. Count: 12→10→8→6→4→2→0 — that's 6 jumps. So 12 ÷ 2 = 6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.1 'Divide by 2' pp.265-268
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Halving and Doubling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Halving Rule
learn.content: Dividing by 2 always gives you half the number. Half of 18 = 9, so 18 ÷ 2 = 9. You can also think of it as: 2 × quotient = dividend. This works for ALL even numbers.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.1 'Divide by 2' pp.265-268
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Halving and Doubling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 절반 루틴 — ÷2는 반으로 자르기
learn.content: ÷2 = 반으로. 즉 두 그룹으로 똑같이 나누기. 예: 14 ÷ 2 → 14의 반 = 7. 예: 18 ÷ 2 → 18의 반 = 9. 단계 1) 짝수인지 확인 (이번 단원은 모두 짝수). 단계 2) 두 그룹으로 나눠 한 쪽 세기 = 답. 팁: 두 자리 짝수 → 십의 자리 ÷ 2 + 일의 자리 ÷ 2. 예: 16 ÷ 2 = (10 ÷ 2) + (6 ÷ 2) = 5 + 3 = 8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.1 'Divide by 2' pp.265-268
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Halving and Doubling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 더블의 역연산 — ÷2는 ×2의 반대
learn.content: 더블(×2)을 알면 ÷2도 즉답. 예: 2 × 7 = 14 → 14 ÷ 2 = 7. 예: 2 × 9 = 18 → 18 ÷ 2 = 9. 곱셈 사실로 사고: 14 ÷ 2 = ? → '몇 × 2 = 14?' → 7. 흔한 실수 (M06): 곱셈으로 풀 줄 알면서도 ÷2에서 다시 그림 — '더블 짝꿍'을 항상 활용.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.1 'Divide by 2' pp.265-268
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Halving and Doubling
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 절반 검증 — 답 + 답 = 원래 수?
learn.content: ÷2 답을 구한 뒤 검산: 답 + 답 = 원래 수? 예: 18 ÷ 2 = 9 → 검산: 9 + 9 = 18 ✓. 예: 14 ÷ 2 = 7 → 검산: 7 + 7 = 14 ✓. 또는 답 × 2 = 원래 수? (곱셈 검산). 🔍 두 가지 검산이 모두 통과해야 정답.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

2 groups of 8 = 16 total. So Tom started with 16 coins.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 503 − 167.
practice_r2.explanation: 503−167: 3−7 borrow → 13−7=6; tens 9−6=3 (after lending across zero); hundreds 4−1=3. Result: 336.

practice_r3.explanation: A=7, B=7, C=7, D=8. A, B, C all equal 7.

feedback.correct: Correct! A=7, B=7, C=7, D=8. A, B, and C all equal 7.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. 14÷2=7, 21÷3=7, 28÷4=7, but 16÷2=8. So A, B, C all equal 7.

practice_r3.question: Which three division problems have the same answer? A) 14÷2  B) 21÷3  C) 28÷4  D) 16÷2
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! A=7, B=7, C=7, D=8. A, B, and C all equal 7.
